description_long: This object consists of two large, rectangular stone blocks set side by side on a shared base, each featuring a prominent circular medallion with geometric relief patterns on the front face. The medallions are bordered by a rope-like motif and contain symmetrical, cross-like designs within. The sides of the blocks are rough and unfinished, while the rear faces display rectangular indentations and weathered surfaces, suggesting age and exposure. The tops are flat and slightly uneven, with visible chisel marks and natural stone texture. The base is broad and stable, supporting the weight of the blocks and providing a solid foundation. The overall appearance is robust and decorative, likely serving as a boundary or ornamental barrier in an urban or historical setting.
description: A pair of large, decorative stone blocks with circular medallion carvings on the front, set on a shared base with rough sides and weathered backs.
description_view_0: Front view showing two stone blocks with detailed circular medallion carvings and rope-like borders.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thickness of the blocks, rough stone texture, and the shared base.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying rectangular indentations and weathered, less ornate surfaces on the backs of the blocks.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, highlighting the block thickness and rough stone finish.
attribute_location_context: historic urban plaza or park boundary
attribute_installation_method: set on a prepared stone or concrete base
attribute_weathering_pattern: Visible surface weathering with some discoloration and minor chipping, especially on the rear and edges.
attribute_presence_of_markings_or_engrav: Prominent geometric carvings and medallion reliefs on the front faces.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: Aligned side by side, likely forming part of a continuous boundary or decorative barrier.
